fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Arkansas Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Arkansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity arkansas, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to arkansas, respiratory therapist compact arkansas, arkansas respiratory therapist endorsement application

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Arkansas.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited Program
steps.description: Complete a respiratory care educational program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC).

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination.

steps.title: Apply for an Arkansas Respiratory Therapist License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, required documentation, and the applicable fee to the Arkansas State Medical Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Arkansas respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Arkansas have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Arkansas. To work in Arkansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Arkansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arkansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Arkansas
hero.dek: Arkansas Respiratory Therapist license requires graduation from an accredited respiratory care program, passing the NBRC examination, and completion of continuing education hours.
